channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rotimi Salami talks about raising a child even if DNA proves he’s not the father
item.description: Nollywood actor Rotimi Salami don open mouth on one matter wey dey touch paternity and how e dey handle family. For interview wey e do with BBC Yoruba, Salami yan say even if DNA test show say pikin n...

item.title: Warriors' Young Squad Earns Praise from Slater After Canberra Win
item.description: The Warriors' young players have caught the eye of former NRL star Billy Slater following their commanding performance against the Canberra Raiders. Despite being a relatively inexperienced squad, the...

item.title: Over 800 Citizen Concerns Addressed via Health Ministry's 'Talk to the Minister' Platform
item.description: Dar es Salaam – More than 800 issues from citizens and health workers have been addressed through the digital 'Talk to the Minister' platform, the Ministry of Health has confirmed. Health Minister Moh...

item.title: Legal Protections for Pakistanis Detained in Bahrain Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: On March 10, Bahrain authorities arrested six people from Asian countries, including five Pakistanis, for allegedly filming and sharing videos about the Iran conflict, which has escalated into a Middl...

item.title: US and Israel Intensify Airstrikes as Iran Rejects Ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Israel have escalated their military campaign against Iran, carrying out what US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth called the 'most intense day of strikes' yet. Tehran residents rep...

item.title: Nehal Hashmi appointed as Sindh Governor following PM recommendation
item.description: On Friday, Nehal Hashmi, a senior PML-N leader, officially took the oath as the new governor of Sindh. The ceremony was held in Karachi and conducted by Sindh High Court Chief Justice Zafar Ahmed Rajp...

item.title: Concerns grow over IFC-backed gas projects and their impact on Africa’s energy transition
item.description: The article by Karabo Mokgonyana critiques the International Finance Corporation (IFC), the private-sector branch of the World Bank, for supporting two new fossil gas projects in Africa while the cont...

item.title: BTO4PBAT27 wraps up second phase of community mobilisation in Akure North, plans third phase
item.description: The BTO4PBAT27 Support Group don successfully wrap up the second phase of their grassroots mobilisation tour for Akure North Local Government Area. This phase na part of their effort to engage communi...

item.title: Immediate Closure of Canberra Autism Centre Leaves Families Struggling
item.description: The AEIOU Foundation centre in Garran, Canberra, which provided early intervention programs and therapy for autistic children, has closed immediately, leaving families and staff in shock. Established ...

item.title: Tiruchi college hostels adjust meals and gas usage amid cylinder shortage
item.description: Educational institutions in Tiruchirapalli are facing a temporary shortage of commercial gas cylinders, which are essential for running hostel messes. Colleges have started adapting to this situation ...

item.title: Canadian Hospitals Overwhelmed: Addressing Emergency Room Wait Times and Systemic Issues
item.description: Canada's hospital emergency rooms are experiencing a severe crisis, with patients facing increasingly long wait times and, in some cases, life-threatening situations. Across the country, patients are ...

item.title: Controversy Surrounds Noma L.A. Pop-Up Amid Abuse Allegations and High Prices
item.description: Noma L.A., a pop-up restaurant in Silver Lake by Danish chef René Redzepi, opened amid significant controversy. Offering multi-course meals at $1,500 per person, the high price has drawn criticism fro...

item.title: ASEAN urges immediate stop to Middle East conflict to protect regional economies
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – ASEAN foreign and economic ministers convened on March 13, 2026, to call for an immediate halt to the war in the Middle East, citing rising oil prices and disrupted trade as seri...

item.title: Netflix Confirms Sequel for Hit Animated Film 'KPop Demon Hunters'
item.description: Netflix has officially approved a sequel to 'KPop Demon Hunters,' the animated film that became the platform's most-watched movie ever. The first movie, released in June 2025, follows a girl group nam...

item.title: Lionesses Squad Announced for World Rugby 7s Division 2 with Auma Back and Adhiambo Out
item.description: NAIROBI — Kenya’s national women’s rugby 7s team, the Lionesses, have announced their squad for the upcoming Division 2 tournament in Montevideo, Uruguay, happening on March 21-22. Impala RFC’s Judith...

item.title: Queensland communities face rising floodwaters as emergency persists
item.description: Queensland is facing a significant flooding emergency, with towns such as Longreach being particularly threatened by rapidly rising water levels. Authorities have warned that record water levels may b...

item.title: Senate To Consult IMF On Nigeria’s Economic Plans And Reforms
item.description: The Nigerian Senate don arrange high-level meeting with IMF officials as part of discussions on Nigeria’s economic situation under President Bola Tinubu. Dis one dey under IMF Article IV Consultation,...

item.title: Understanding LPG Usage in Cooking Cylinders and Alternatives with PNG
item.description: The article discusses why liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) is predominantly used in cooking gas cylinders in India, and whether piped natural gas (PNG) can serve as a viable alternative. LPG has been the...

item.title: Statistics Canada to release latest February employment and unemployment report
item.description: Statistics Canada is preparing to release its official February 2026 jobs and unemployment data, a key indicator for understanding the country’s economic direction as it faces ongoing global uncertain...

item.title: Heavy Rain Forces Cancellation of SS3 Stage in Safari Rally
item.description: The organisers of the 2026 WRC Safari Rally have cancelled the SS3 Camp Moran 2 stage that was meant to open Day Two of the rally due to heavy rains that made the route impassable. Instead, the action...
